t_ch: 请讲吧。
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 这是一个颇为激烈的故事……
t_en: This is rather intense...

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Something came up.

t_ch: 你先去忙吧，我再和它僵持一会儿！就不信这鸟不肯开口！
t_en: Off you go and do that, then. I'll try and continue to get it to talk. I can't believe this bird refuses to talk!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，要听故事吗？我还有很多故事可以讲给你听。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, would you like to hear a story? I've got many more stories I could tell you.

t_ch: 聆听「游辞纪事」
t_en: Listen to "The Chronicles of Youci."

t_ch: 进入「奇珍琳琅」
t_en: Enter "Priceless Jewels."

t_ch: 有没有不那么「刺激」的故事？
t_en: Do you have any stories that aren't so "intense"?

t_ch: 我有很多其他故事，但并非为你而准备。若你想听，我也可以讲，只是这并不能使你获得奖赏。
t_en: I've got lots of other stories, but they're not just for you. I can tell you the stories, if you'd like — you just won't be rewarded for it.

t_ch: 但既然你如此好奇，那听听这个故事吧……
t_en: But seeing as you're so curious, then why don't you give this story a listen...

t_ch: 有些问题想问你…
t_en: I've got a few questions I'd like to ask you...

t_ch: 请尽管问吧。
t_en: Please, ask away.

t_ch: 你究竟是什么？
t_en: What exactly are you?

t_ch: 我与你一样，只是一个受造物。
t_en: Just like you, I'm a creation.

t_ch: 你有什么目的？
t_en: What do you want?

t_ch: 我与你一样，防止银河走向它的命运/必然/结局/单一的确定系统/前定和谐。
t_en: Just like you, I prevent the cosmos from trending toward its own destiny/inevitability/conclusion/single fixed system/predestined harmony.

t_ch: 你为什么只和我说话？
t_en: Why do you only talk with me?

t_ch: 正如之前所言，你牵动着银河的潮汐。
t_en: As mentioned, you impel the tides of the cosmos.

t_ch: 我没其他问题了。
t_en: No further questions.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 游辞沉默不语。
t_en: Youci remains silent.

t_ch: 我见过你的宠物了。
t_en: I've met your pet already.

t_ch: 啊，您见过在下家的游辞了～怎么样？是不是很有意思？
t_en: Ah, you met my little Youci~ How about it? Isn't it fascinating?

t_ch: 是挺有意思的。
t_en: Really, quite fascinating.

t_ch: 是吧！在下就料到您会喜欢它。
t_en: Indeed! I knew you'd take a liking to it.

t_ch: 没啥意思…
t_en: Not at all...

t_ch: 没啥意思？唉，也不奇怪…无名客还是见多识广啊，在下手里的小玩意儿是满足不了您咯·～
t_en: Not at all? *sigh* That's no surprise... The Nameless is vastly experienced. Nothing I have in my humble possession could hope to satisfy you~

t_ch: 其实挺诡异的。
t_en: Actually, it was pretty weird.

t_ch: 哈哈哈！确实如此。在下倒是觉得，这份「诡异」恰恰是它最有趣的地方。
t_en: Hahaha, indeed! I actually think this "weirdness" is what's most fascinating about it.

t_ch: 那您有没有遇到什么…特殊情况？
t_en: So then, have you encountered any... special circumstances?

t_ch: 它和我对话了。
t_en: It chatted with me.

t_ch: 啊？他和您对话了？…这…看来她没有骗我，这只鸟确实…别在意，在下自言自语罢了。
t_en: Ah? It chatted with you? ...This... looks like she wasn't lying. This bird can actually... Don't mind me. I'm just talking to myself.

t_ch: 还有没有别的什么事？比如…给你什么东西？
t_en: Was there anything else? Such as... Did it give you anything?

t_ch: 它给了我一片羽毛。
t_en: It gave me a feather.

t_ch: 啊哈，在下就料到它会给您留下这羽毛！这可是好东西啊，您要好好保管。
t_en: Aha, I knew it'd gift a feather to you! They're really good, and you should take good care of it.

t_ch: 这羽毛有什么用？
t_en: What's the feather for?

t_ch: 瞧它轻盈通透，像是某种宝石制成的小物件，就把它拿去卖钱呗～卖一个羽毛能少说好几天书呢！
t_en: Just look at how light and transparent it is, like some small object sculpted from gemstone. Go sell it for money~ One feather is worth all you can earn across several days of storytelling!

t_ch: 您有空时，多去找它聊聊天吧…在下平时工作比较忙，也担心让它太寂寞。既然它唯独肯和您对话，那也许它还挺喜欢您的呢。
t_en: When you have the time, spare some time and chat with it more... I often find myself busy with work, and I fear it grows lonely. Seeing as it only chats with you, perhaps it has taken a liking to you?

t_ch: 况且再怎么说，这些羽毛可是实打实的好东西呀～
t_en: And besides, those feathers most assuredly are REALLY good~

t_ch: 她选择与父亲战斗…
t_en: She chooses to fight her father...

t_ch: 冰冷的利爪划过脊背…
t_en: Cold, sharp claws rake across his back...

t_ch: 他发现星神并不存在…
t_en: He discovers that Aeons do not in fact exist...

t_ch: 再讲一个故事吧！
t_en: Tell us another story!

t_ch: 这鸟太有意思了！
t_en: This bird is really fascinating!

t_ch: 能再讲个更吓人的吗？
t_en: Can you give us a horror story?

t_ch: 你也跟我说说话嘛！
t_en: Chat with me, too!

t_ch: 为什么不跟我说话呢？
t_en: Why aren't you talking to me?

t_ch: 真是只脾气古怪的鸟……
t_en: What a strange and moody bird...

t_ch: 我有很多其他故事，但并非是为这个宇宙而准备。若你想听，我也可以讲，只是这并不能使你获得奖赏。
t_en: I've got lots of other stories, but they're just not ready for this universe. I can tell you the stories, if you'd like — you just won't be rewarded for it.

t_ch: 但既然你如此好奇，那听听这个故事吧……
t_en: But seeing as you're so curious, then why don't you give this story a listen...

t_ch: 银河中有个叫做「贝洛伯格」的末日孤城，风雪已经笼罩了它数个琥珀纪。据史料记载，那是某位筑城者为了战胜反物质军团的攻势，用禁忌手段召唤来的无尽冰霜。
t_en: Deep in the cosmos lies a doomed outpost city called Belobog, enveloped by a blizzard for several Amber Eras now. Historical records show this to be inexhaustible coldness summoned through unspeakable means by one of the Architects to repel an assault by the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 而如今，风雪突如其来地消退了。城市变得温暖宜居，生命也开始复苏。有人说，这是几位英雄用某种方式停止了风雪，使贝洛伯格得以重生。
t_en: But now, the blizzard has subsided as fast as it came. The city has become warm and vibrant, and life has started to return. Some say that it was several heroes who, in some way, managed to revive Belobog.

t_ch: 可实际上，那是绝灭大君「星啸」在风雪毁灭她军团前就埋下的伏笔。一颗绝灭大君的「种子」被安放在风雪之外，等待着再次君临世界的契机。
t_en: But in fact, this was all foretold by the Lord Ravager Celenova just prior to her legion being wiped out by the blizzard. A "seed" was planted beyond the blizzard by the Lord Ravager, dormant until the next chance to rule the world.

t_ch: 当贝洛伯格的人们正沉浸在万物复苏的喜悦中时，忽然有人看到，那些被冰封的虚卒重新苏醒，在残存的雪原中列队集结。
t_en: While the people of Belobog were lost in the joy of the revival, someone suddenly noticed the frozen Voidrangers started to reawaken, assembling into formations upon whatever remained of the Snow Plains.

t_ch: 星啸埋下的种子，此刻已经发芽了。她令风雪消退、寒冰融化，令那些本应永远沉睡的虚卒再度苏醒，展开复仇。
t_en: The seed planted by Celenova had begun to sprout. She drove away the blizzard and melted away the ice, allowing those Voidrangers, once eternally frozen, to reawaken to exact cold vengeance.

t_ch: 那些虚卒跪在地上，向这位令人恐惧的领袖宣誓效忠，并齐声呼喊着她那令人战栗的名号——
t_en: The Voidrangers dropped to one knee, swearing fealty to their feared leader as they chanted together her devastating name:

t_ch: 「漆黑的虎克大人！！！」
t_en: "Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!!!"

t_ch: 故事到这里就结束了，因为没有人亲眼看到接下来发生了什么样的「毁灭」。希望你能喜欢这段不为人知的秘史。
t_en: Our story ends here for now, because nobody could survive to see what "Destruction" followed. I hope you've enjoyed this unknown slice of secret history.

t_ch: 我有很多其他故事，但并非是为这个宇宙而准备。若你想听，我也可以讲，只是这并不能使你获得奖赏。
t_en: I've got lots of other stories, but they're just not ready for this universe. I can tell you the stories, if you'd like — you just won't be rewarded for it.

t_ch: 但既然你如此好奇，那听听这个故事吧……
t_en: But seeing as you're so curious, then why don't you give this story a listen...

t_ch: 几乎每个人都知道，罗浮的「将军」乃是景元。可只有极少数人清楚，实际上统御罗浮、做出决策的另有其人。景元不过是明面上的将军，那位阴影中的将军才是真正的天人之总。
t_en: Almost everyone knows that the General of the Luofu is Jing Yuan. But only a few know about the other person actually making the decisions and controlling the Luofu. Jing Yuan is merely a general to fool the public. It's that secret shadow general who is really running the show.

t_ch: 此人平时不显山不露水，将自己隐藏于市井之中。人们都认为，她不过是一介无权无势的升斗小民。
t_en: This person is someone who always keeps their cards super close, hiding in plain sight at the marketplace. Everyone takes her for just a powerless peasant living hand to mouth.

t_ch: 当然，这样是最安全的。无论是丰饶令使还是绝灭大君，都不太可能精准找到这位影子将军的存在。
t_en: Naturally, this is the most secure option. Be it an Abundance Emanator or a Lord Ravager, it was impossible to accurately determine the identity of this shadow general.

t_ch: 影子将军擅长卜算吉凶，但却不屑于以此趋吉避凶。在她看来，趋吉避凶乃是庸人所为，想要赢得大，就要赌得多、不怕输。
t_en: The shadow general was a skilled diviner, but would not stoop to using the art for personal gain. In her estimation, seeking luck and avoiding misfortune is what mediocre people do. If you want to win big, you have to bet big — and not be afraid of losing.

t_ch: 譬如那日，联盟集结兵力，协助博识学会清缴一处星区内的孽物。在尝试突破造翼者佣兵的封锁时，罗浮太卜符玄认为，应当集结优势兵力，进攻薄弱处。
t_en: For example, on that day, an alliance was formed to assist the Intelligentsia Guild in purging abominations within a star zone. While attempting to break through the Wingweaver blockade, Fu Xuan — the Luofu's master diviner — believed that because their rallied forces were superior they should assault the weakest link.

t_ch: 但影子将军却说：「不，我们应当进攻他们防守最严密的部分。」六御大都知道影子将军强硬的性格，没人敢提出任何异议。
t_en: But the shadow general countered: "No, we should assault their most strongly defended part." Fully aware of the shadow general's hardcore nature, none of the Six Charioteers dared raise any objection.

t_ch: 只有符玄战战兢兢地说道：「青雀大人，敌人准备充分，如果贸然攻击防守严密处，反而会让我军陷入包围……」
t_en: Only Fu Xuan inquired, trembling with trepidation: "Esteemed Qingque, the enemy is well-prepared. If we attack a strongly defended garrison in haste, our forces risk becoming surrounded..."

t_ch: 影子将军一边玩着手中的琼玉牌，一边笑着说：「小玄子，我们打个赌吧。我赌他们的枪里没有子弹。」
t_en: The shadow general smiled as she flitted a jade tile between her fingers, and said: "Oh, Fu-Fu — let's make a bet. I bet there's no bullets in their guns."

t_ch: 事实证明，因为步离人和造翼者的内讧，那支佣兵军团的后勤补给已经完全崩溃。
t_en: It turns out bad blood between the borisin and Wingweavers had completely collapsed logistics supplies for this mercenary legion.

t_ch: 「防守薄弱处」实际上是他们仅存的有生力量，而看起来防守最严密的地方，则是无数残兵败将在硬撑门面，妄想能骗过联盟军。
t_en: The "weakest link" was actually their only remaining strength, while what seemed to be the most heavily defended part was actually where all the scattered remnants of the officers lurked in a vain attempt to deceive the alliance.

t_ch: 可惜青将军不愿抛头露面，她这般英明神武，也久久不为人知，只能成为街头巷尾的传说。
t_en: It's a great pity that General Qingque was never willing to show her face in public. Her wisdom and power went unknown for so long, that in the end she could only become an urban legend.

t_ch: 我有很多其他故事，但并非是为这个宇宙而准备。若你想听，我也可以讲，只是这并不能使你获得奖赏。
t_en: I've got lots of other stories, but they're just not ready for this universe. I can tell you the stories, if you'd like — you just won't be rewarded for it.

t_ch: 但既然你如此好奇，那听听这个故事吧……
t_en: But seeing as you're so curious, then why don't you give this story a listen...

t_ch: 即使在很多琥珀纪以后，贝洛伯格的人们也会时常想起那场世纪大审判——代理大守护者佩拉审判僭主布洛妮娅。
t_en: Even after many Amber Eras, the citizens of Belobog will still harken to the trial of the era: The trial of the tyrant, Bronya, by the acting Supreme Guardian, Pela.

t_ch: 这案情的确离奇：下任大守护者从雪原中回来，说前任大守护者可可利亚已经背叛了贝洛伯格，并被自己就地正法。
t_en: The circumstances behind this case are indeed bizarre: The incoming Supreme Guardian came back from the Snow Plains, claiming that the previous Supreme Guardian, Cocolia, had betrayed Belobog — and thus summarily got executed on the spot.

t_ch: 当时在现场，能够证明布洛妮娅所言非虚的证人有抵抗组织的干部和外星的通缉犯。此外还有两位虽不在现场但算是亲历者的人，是被可可利亚剥夺权力的落马官员和她的弟弟。
t_en: On the scene at the time, those who could testify that what Bronya said was true included cadres from the resistance movement and some aliens with outstanding warrants. Besides those, there were two people absent who were also witnesses, however: The fallen official stripped of power by Cocolia, and her little brother.

t_ch: 因为每一位证人都有杀害可可利亚的动机，所以自然没几个人肯相信布洛妮娅的鬼话。她很快被佩拉推翻，后者举行了一场关乎贝城命运的大审判。
t_en: Scarcely anyone was willing to believe Bronya's nonsense, because each of the witnesses had their own motive for wanting to kill Cocolia. She was quickly vanquished by Pela, who staged a grand trial to determine the fate of Belobog.

t_ch: 在庭审中，越来越多的证据被提出，布洛妮娅证言的真实性开始得到验证。如果按照正常的审判流程，临时军事法庭应该能在几个月内得出结论。
t_en: During the trial, evidence mounted up and the veracity of Bronya's claims started to become verified. Were normal procedures to be followed, a provisional military court should be able to reach a deliberation within a few months or so.

t_ch: 但民众间的对立和利益集团间的博弈，并没有给法庭得出结论的时间。在审判开始的第七天，内战爆发了。
t_en: But division among the general public and interest groups competing with each other meant the court had no time to reach a conclusion. On the seventh day of the trial, civil war erupted.

t_ch: 也许在内战时，大多数人就已经清楚知道布洛妮娅没有说谎了。但事情发展到这个地步，「真相」早就没有意义了。
t_en: Perhaps during the trial, most people had already figured out that Bronya wasn't lying. But once things reach this stage, the truth no longer holds any water.

t_ch: 等到星际和平公司介入调停，并将雅利洛-VI纳入自己的贸易体系时，有很多有识之士悔不当初。但此时又能怎么办呢？
t_en: Once IPC intervened with mediation and brought Jarilo-VI into its trading network, many of those in-the-know came to regret it. But what could be done now?

t_ch: 这便是雅利洛-VI近代史中的一页。希望你能喜欢。
t_en: This is one page in a chapter on the modern history of Jarilo-VI. I hope you've enjoyed it.

t_ch: 游辞的声音如细雨中飘摇着的纱裙般轻盈，可你却在故事开始的一瞬间，坠入五里雾中…恍惚间，你仿佛陷入了一场恶战。
t_en: Youci's voice is as light as a silk skirt dancing in the soft drizzle, and yet you fall into an impenetrable fog the moment the story starts... Dazed, you find yourself to be engaged in a fierce battle.

t_ch: 伟大、尊贵、永世光荣的嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀阁下，恭迎您出征归来。
t_en: Welcome back, Your Excellency, the esteemed and illustrious Lord Tick-Tock.

t_ch: 遇见您是我此生莫大的幸运。我该用什么来赞美太阳？绚丽的糖浆因您的出现而黯然失色，简陋的酒吧因您的到来而熠熠生辉，在我心中，您就像那——
t_en: It is my utmost fortune to have encountered your presence. How shall I extol the sun? Your radiance surpasses the splendor of syrup, and the humble lounge gleams with your arrival. In my heart, you are akin to the...

t_ch: 苏乐达的瓶盖！若是失去了您，整瓶汽水…不，所有快乐都将毫无意义。
t_en: SoulGlad's bottle cap! Without you, the entire bottle of SoulGlad... No, all joy would lose its meaning.

t_ch: …是不是人设搞错了？
t_en: ...What's up with this guy?

t_ch: 它不是这么说话的吧？
t_en: Wait, that's not how he usually talks, right?

t_ch: 很好！我忠实的家仆哟，我正面临选择。请用你那足以昭示未来的舞步，来为我揭晓——究竟什么样的饮品，才配得上这一路的颠沛流离。
t_en: Well said, my loyal servant! Now, I find myself at a crossroads. Show me the answer through your dance steps that portend the future: Which beverage befits this extraordinary journey?

t_ch: 您的意愿就是我的意志。
t_en: Your will is my command, Your Excellency.

t_ch: …嗯。嗯。多面骰的花色显示，今日最适宜的饮品，当由那边的调饮师亲手制作。
t_en: ...Hmm. Uh-huh. Yes, the hues of the dice indicate that the finest beverage shall be handcrafted by the drinksmith over there.

t_ch: ——那边？可笑！那个酒保不过是我从村口旅馆捡回来的，一介村姑，连父母都找不到，你说她能调出我需要的饮料？哈哈，我倒真要看看！
t_en: The drinksmith over there? Preposterous! She is naught but a village girl I found from the inn. You believe an ignorant girl, who cannot even trace her lineage, to possess the skill to brew the beverage I require? Ha! How intriguing!

t_ch: 喂，给我……
t_en: Hey, bring me...

t_ch: 老爷！老爷！——大事不好了！
t_en: Master...! Terrible news, Master!

t_ch: <size=28>夫人它…它又离家出走了！</size>
t_en: <size=28>It's Madam! She... she's run away again!</size>

t_ch: 跑了多远？
t_en: Where did she go?

t_ch: 还没走出酒吧。
t_en: She's still inside the lounge.

t_ch: 那没事。你去告诉它，我回来了，让它速速迎接。
t_en: Well, no need to make a commotion. Inform her of my return and request her immediate presence.

t_ch: 我禀告过了，可夫人不听！它还说…还说……
t_en: I've told Madam, but she wouldn't listen! She even said... that...

t_ch: 它怎么说？
t_en: What did she say?

t_ch: 它说…它要在酒吧里当调饮师，再也不回去了！！！
t_en: She said... she's going to be a drinksmith and will never come back!!!

t_ch: 啊？岂有此理！我去亲自问问它！
t_en: What? How outrageous! I shall personally inquire of her!

t_ch: 话音未落，司令突然从远处惊呼着跑来。
t_en: Before the sentence was finished, Mr. Bigwig suddenly ran over from a distance, shouting in alarm.

t_ch: 对不起。我不是有意要瞒着您，只是…打算给您一个惊喜。
t_en: I beg your pardon. I did not intend to keep it concealed from you, I merely wished to... bring you a surprise.

t_ch: 听说酒吧里来了位技艺高超的调饮师，我想为您接风洗尘，所以就跑来了这边，希望能学到调出美味饮料的诀窍。不要生我的气，好不好嘛。
t_en: I have heard of a skilled drinksmith at the lounge, and I desired to pay homage to your greatness. Hence, I came here intending to learn how to craft delicious beverages. Please, I implore you, do not be angered with me.

t_ch: 您看。在您远征期间，我收集了好多关于您丰功伟绩的报道。
t_en: During your expedition, I gathered numerous accounts detailing your magnificent achievements.

t_ch: 「…让我们瞧瞧，是哪位英雄率领美梦剧团，赢得了对垃圾桶王国的胜利？」
t_en: "...Behold! Which great hero led the Sweet Dreams Troupe to triumph against the Trashcan Kingdom?"

t_ch: 「…是哪位英雄，将不可一世的塔塔洛夫踩在脚下，高举自由平等的旗帜？」
t_en: "...Which hero trampled the presumptuous Tatalov beneath their feet and raised the banner of freedom and equality?"

t_ch: 「我们问——它是谁？汽水瓶回答：嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀元帅！」
t_en: "We inquire, who is it? The soda bottles reply: It's His Excellency, Marshal Tick-Tock!"

t_ch: 「我们问——它是谁？广告牌回答：嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀元帅！」
t_en: "We inquire, who is it? The billboards reply: It's His Excellency, Marshal Tick-Tock!"

t_ch: 「我们问——它是谁？整个梦境都在回答：嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀元帅！」
t_en: "We inquire, who is it? Replies the entire Dreamscape: It's His Excellency, Marshal Tick-Tock!"

t_ch: 我也会我也会！刚才为您现场做了首诗，请您欣赏。
t_en: Me, too! Me too! I just wrote a poem for you. Please enjoy it!

t_ch: 这就是嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀将军！英勇作战无人可挡，文治武功举世无双。有深谋远虑，有胆识，还能冲阵，有力量，甚至独战塔塔洛夫！然后就……
t_en: Behold, the renowned General Tick-Tock! In battle invincible, wisdom profound! Valiant and skilled, a warrior's might! Conquering his foe Tatalov, with his valorous light!...

t_ch: ——哈哈哈哈哈。过奖！没想到我的威名传颂得如此之广，这才是该有的样子！
t_en: Hahahahahahaha. Such adulation! I was unaware that my renown had spread so far and wide, yet it is only befitting!

t_ch: 不过啊，我跟你说，那个调饮师不行的，就是个我捡回来的孤儿，擦擦桌子还可以，指望她调出点有意思的东西，那不太可能。
t_en: However, let me enlighten you: That drinksmith is unsuitable. She is but an orphan I took in. She may tend to the tables, yet her ability to create anything of interest is doubtful.

t_ch: 不信，你们看——
t_en: If you doubt my words, you...

t_ch: …晚、晚上好，嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀先生。
t_en: ...G—Good evening, Mr. Tick-Tock.

t_ch: 真不把我上次教你的东西当回事啊，说话时要低下头，双手摆正，抬起眼睛看我！
t_en: Looks like you've failed to take heed of the lessons I taught you. When you speak, bow your head, extend your hands, and look up to me!

t_ch: 跟你说了多少遍对客人要礼貌，你哪次做到了？
t_en: How often have I instructed you to display courtesy to the guests!? But have you ever complied?

t_ch: 我…抱歉冒犯了您。小女子才刚到酒吧不久，还不熟悉这里的规矩。请您原谅。
t_en: I... I beg your pardon for my transgression. I've newly arrived at the lounge, and I'm unfamiliar with the rules here. I implore your pardon.

t_ch: 喂喂，这个错得更离谱了啊！
t_en: Hey! Something is definitely wrong with her!

t_ch: …你到底是有多害怕舒翁啊。
t_en: ...Just how much are you afraid of Siobhan?

t_ch: 唉，跟你这样没教养的人计较也是白费劲。给我调杯饮料吧，也让新来的见识见识——我，堂堂嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀爵士的专属调饮师，究竟有多优秀！
t_en: Alas, debating with such an ill-bred servant is a waste of time. Well, bring me beverages and showcase the extraordinary talents of the personal drinksmith in the esteemed service of Sir Tick-Tock, the Illustrious One!

t_ch: 其实…听闻您远征归来，小女子早就准备好了庆功的特调，请您品尝。
t_en: In truth, upon learning of your return from your expedition, I have prepared a commemorative beverage for Your Excellency.

t_ch: 不错！算你有心。来！大声告诉其他人，你打算为这杯光荣的饮料，取什么名字？
t_en: Not bad at all! Come forth and announce with honor! What shall this glorious beverage be named?

t_ch: 「败逃的提督」。
t_en: The Defeated Governor.

t_ch: 啊？这是在讽刺我吗？！
t_en: What? Are you being sarcastic!?

t_ch: …请您息怒。取名采用了欲扬先抑的手法，而这款饮品的灵感，则源自您华丽的外观。
t_en: ...Please, calm your ire. The name was chosen to make a statement, and the beverage was inspired by your resplendent countenance.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>底层的黑色</color>，代表激烈残酷的战场；<color=#dbc291ff>表层的白色</color>，代表无路可退的绝望；而<color=#dbc291ff>中间的蓝色</color>，则是您沉着冷静的意志。
t_en: The <color=#dbc291ff>black</color> layer at the base signifies a fierce and savage battlefield. The <color=#dbc291ff>white</color> layer at the top denotes despair. And the <color=#dbc291ff>blue</color> layer in the middle represents your composure and unyielding resolve.

t_ch: 即使战至最后一人，也仍然斗志激昂，独自在漆黑的荒野中开拓出应当前进的道路。
t_en: Even when you stand as the sole survivor, you retain the will to forge a path through the darkest wilderness.

t_ch: 说得好！看看，我捡回来的调饮师都这么有涵养，仆从们，都学着点啊！
t_en: Well said! Look, even the drinksmith I took in is well-cultured. Learn from her, my servants!

t_ch: 喂，新来的！我们去找个地方坐下，你把那杯饮料拿过来。搞快点！
t_en: Hey, newcomer! Find a befitting seat for me and bring my beverage! Hurry!

t_ch: …人家很好奇呀，那杯饮料真的会有效果嘛？
t_en: ...I'm curious. Will that drink really work?

t_ch: 调饮就这么有意思，都舍不得回家了？
t_en: Is mixing beverages so much fun that you wouldn't want to go home?

t_ch: 呀！
t_en: Yikes!

t_ch: （舒翁动过手脚的饮料应该就是这个…咦，怎么有两杯？）
t_en: (This must be the drink that Siobhan tampered with... Wait, two glasses?)

t_ch: （…先都拿过去吧。）
t_en: (...Well, I'll take them both.)

t_ch: 太慢了！再说，哪有服务员在工作时间玩手机。尊重在哪里！礼仪在哪里！对我身份的敬畏又在哪里！
t_en: Too sluggish! What kind of waiters indulge in cellphones during working hours? Show some respect! Where are your manners? Show deference in light of my presence!

t_ch: 把饮料给我！…哈？怎么拿了两杯过来，你连颜色都分不清吗？！
t_en: Bring me my drink!... Huh? Why two glasses? Can't you even tell the colors apart!?

t_ch: （赶快回想下，舒翁特意准备的饮品是哪杯。）
t_en: (I must recall which drink is the one Siobhan "specially" prepared.)

t_ch: 底层是黑色？
t_en: Is the bottom layer black?

t_ch: 中间是黑色？
t_en: Is the middle layer black?

t_ch: （好像不太对，再想想吧。）
t_en: (No, that doesn't seem right. Think again.)

t_ch: 中间是蓝色？
t_en: Is the middle layer blue?

t_ch: 表层是黑色？
t_en: Is the top layer black?

t_ch: （好像不太对，再想想吧。）
t_en: (No, that doesn't seem right. Think again.)

t_ch: 表层是蓝色？
t_en: Is the top layer blue?

t_ch: （好像不太对，再想想吧。）
t_en: (No, that doesn't seem right. Think again.)

t_ch: 表层是白色？
t_en: Is the top layer white?

t_ch: （就是这杯饮料了，快递给它吧。）
t_en: (That's the drink. Now I should deliver it.)

t_ch: 中间是白色？
t_en: Is the middle layer white?

t_ch: （好像不太对，再想想吧。）
t_en: (No, that doesn't seem right. Think again.)

t_ch: 底层是白色？
t_en: Is the bottom layer white?

t_ch: （好像不太对，再想想吧。）
t_en: (No, that doesn't seem right. Think again.)

t_ch: 底层是蓝色？
t_en: Is the bottom layer blue?

t_ch: （好像不太对，再想想吧。）
t_en: (No, that doesn't seem right. Think again.)

t_ch: 好像忘记了…
t_en: I... forgot...

t_ch: （被舒翁狠狠地瞪了一眼……）
t_en: (Siobhan just gave me a black look...)

t_ch: 尊、尊贵的嘀嗒嘀•嗒嘀先生，饮用这杯特调前，请允许小女子再次为您介绍它背后的丰富含义。加深对饮品的理解，相信对您的品鉴会大有帮助。
t_en: V—Venerable Mr. Tick-Tock, please allow me to re-introduce the profound significance behind this special beverage. I firmly believe that deepening your understanding of the drink will enhance your tasting experience.

t_ch: 哼，料你也说不出个所以然来，随便讲讲吧，权当是给我助兴了。
t_en: I have no expectations of you providing anything insightful, but anyway, feel free to express whatever thoughts you may have.

t_ch: 感谢您的仁慈。调制这杯饮品时，小女子选用<color=#dbc291ff>黑色的果液作为基底</color>，喻指激烈残酷的战场；<color=#dbc291ff>纯白的羊乳浮在表层</color>，代表着无路可退的绝望。
t_en: I sincerely appreciate your leniency. In the creation of this beverage, I selected <color=#dbc291ff>black juice as the base layer</color>, symbolizing a fierce and savage battlefield, and <color=#dbc291ff>a layer of white goat's milk to adorn the surface</color>, denoting despair.

t_ch: 而<color=#dbc291ff>中间的蓝色</color>，则是为了彰显您沉着冷静的意志，必将带领我们开辟出新的道路。
t_en: The <color=#dbc291ff>blue layer in the middle</color> represents your composure and unyielding resolve, which shall undoubtedly guide us towards a new path.

t_ch: …记住了吗？
t_en: ...Remember that?

t_ch: （舒翁重新描述了一遍，现在应该能找到那杯正确的饮料了。）
t_en: (Siobhan just described it again. Now I should be able to find the correct drink.)

t_ch: 呼哈——味道真棒！所谓的饮料就是要像这样，包含着尊敬和爱戴才行！看看你之前调的都是些什么玩意，是能给人喝的吗？！
t_en: Aha! A beverage of extraordinary taste! This exemplifies the essence of respect and affection! Look at the beverages you mixed before! Could they ever aspire to grace an honored patron's goblet!?

t_ch: 怎么好像有点发困，饮料的后劲太大了，缓不过来？不应该啊…我明明……
t_en: Wait, why am I feeling a bit sleepy? Is this drink too potent? But this can't be... I'm the...

t_ch: …你醒啦？
t_en: ...You're awake?

t_ch: 嘻嘻。瞧瞧你那倒霉样！整天嚣张得厉害，实际上…嘿，也就在酒吧里逞逞威风！
t_en: Haha, look at that pitiful look on your face! You've been acting all high and mighty here, but guess what?... You're just a nobody in this lounge!

t_ch: 还擅自说人家是你的夫人，可谁会喜欢傻瓜呀。我才不要。
t_en: And you had the audacity to call me your wife? Ha! Who in their right mind would marry a fool like you? Definitely not me.

t_ch: 锵锵！即将上演的是——复仇的戏剧！
t_en: Ta-da! Here comes... the drama of vengeance!

t_ch: 你们…刚才都是装出来的？但…这不是我的梦吗？为什么……
t_en: You... you were just playing along? But... isn't this my dream? So, why...

t_ch: 我是「惊梦酒吧」的主人，那些饮品都是我准备的。所以…你说呢？
t_en: I'm the owner of Dreamjolt Hostelry, and I prepared all those drinks. I'm sure you understand now, right?

t_ch: …是我以前待你太温和了，还是你到叛逆期了？居然敢说我是你捡回来的，胆子可真不小啊……
t_en: ...Did I treat you too gently before, or have you entered your rebellious phase? How dare you say that you plucked me from an inn? You've got some nerve...

t_ch: 生气的舒翁好恐怖哇……
t_en: Siobhan looks so scary when she's angry...

t_ch: 不服管教的孩子可不能惯着…得适当让他们吃点苦头。溺爱可是最糟糕的教育方式，切记，切记。
t_en: I never spoil misbehaving kids... They need to face the consequences. Pampering them is the worst way to teach them, remember that.

t_ch: 后面就交给你们了，别玩得太过火就好。
t_en: I'll leave the rest to you. Just don't go overboard.

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎，就等你这句话！
t_en: Mwahahaha, I've been waiting for this!

t_ch: 可以帮人家把搅拌棒拿过来嘛，想要尝试好久了…嘻嘻。
t_en: Could you please bring me the stirring stick? I've been wanting to try it for quite a while... Hee-hee...

t_ch: 后续情节不适合放映，本场公演到此为止。感谢您的观看。下期再会。
t_en: The subsequent material is not suitable for viewing. This performance ends here. Thanks for watching. See you next time.

t_ch: …我知道错了，我不该欺负其他怪物，不该拿舒翁开玩笑…我说的话都是假的，都是随便闹着玩玩，不作数的！
t_en: ...I'm so sorry! I shouldn't have bullied other monsters or teased Siobhan... I was talking nonsense! It was all just a joke!

t_ch: <size=36>…你们不要过来啊！</size>
t_en: <size=36>...No! Stay away from me!</size>

t_ch: 新人，好好看，好好学。真正优秀的酒吧该是这样。
t_en: Watch and learn, new blood. This is what a good lounge should be like.

t_ch: 端走舒翁的饮品
t_en: Retrieve Siobhan's drink

t_ch: 将饮品递给铁皮人
t_en: Serve the drink to the Tin Man

t_ch: 「这是一枚梦泡。」
t_en: "This is a dream bubble."

t_ch: …我当然知道！
t_en: ...Of course I know that!

t_ch: 不需要你解释
t_en: No need to explain that.

t_ch: （小星星不在身边，它的声音是从哪传来的……）
t_en: (Starlet isn't around. Where is his voice coming from...?)

t_ch: 「酒店里出现梦泡，是梦境正在坍塌的标志。」
t_en: "Dream bubbles appearing in the hotel is a sign that the Dreamscape is collapsing."

t_ch: 「噩梦在扩散。『美梦剧团』受到侵袭而变得狂乱。」
t_en: "The Nightmare is spreading, and the Sweet Dreams Troupe has been corrupted and become frenzied."

t_ch: 「对家族来说，这是必须解决的大麻烦。」
t_en: "For The Family, this is a big problem that must be solved."

t_ch: 「对我们而言，情况或许并非如此。」
t_en: "However, for us, it might not be the same."

t_ch: 你究竟想说什么？
t_en: What are you trying to imply?

t_ch: …你到底在哪里？
t_en: ...Where exactly are you?

t_ch: 「你必须到达<color=#dbc291ff>那个房间</color>。」
t_en: "You must reach <color=#dbc291ff>that room</color>."

t_ch: 「亲身去体验吧。我做了点调整。在这个空间中，你会以我的模样出现。」
t_en: "Go experience it for yourself. I've made a few adjustments, and you'll appear in my form in this space."

t_ch: 「记住：景象或许是虚假的，但你看到的所有事都真实发生过。」
t_en: "Remember, the sights may be false, but everything you're about to see did happen before."

t_ch: 汪！我们来玩真心话大冒险哇！
t_en: Woof! Let's play Truth or Dare!

t_ch: 被点到名的朋友必须从「真心话」和「大冒险」里面选一个！完成挑战后就可以指名下一个怪物！汪…要从谁开始哇？
t_en: The person who gets called must choose between Truth or Dare! Once you've completed the challenge, you can select the next one! Woof... who should we start with?

t_ch: …人家喜欢被动，才不要做先告白的那个呢。
t_en: ...I prefer to be passive. I don't want to be the one who confesses first.

t_ch: 不会欺负我吧？唉，就很郁闷，说是投票，你们肯定都商量好了要选我。
t_en: You're not going to bully me, are you? Well, I know it's a vote, but I'm pretty sure you all agreed to pick on me.

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎，这样就没法玩了。想个办法，想个办法。嘻嘻，要不…就从铁罐头开始吧？
t_en: Mwahaha! That's not how you play. Well, let's think of a way. Hee-hee, how about... we start with that tin can?

t_ch: ——大的要让着小的，大小都一样，没毛病！
t_en: ...The big one should give way to the smaller ones. Nothing wrong with that!

t_ch: 行啊，就让我来给你们做个示范！都看好了啊！
t_en: Alright! Let me show you how it's done! Watch this!

t_ch: 你是要选「真心话」还是「大冒险」哇？
t_en: So, "Truth"? Or "Dare"?

t_ch: 我选…「真心话」！
t_en: I choose... "Truth"!

t_ch: 真没意思！嘿嘿，听我出题——「你最害怕的对象是？」
t_en: That's no fun at all! Hee-hee, alright, I'll ask you a question... Who are you most afraid of?

t_ch: …舒翁。
t_en: ...Siobhan.

t_ch: 汪！这个太耍赖了，不能算数哇！
t_en: Woof! That's cheating! It doesn't count!

t_ch: 怎么就不算？轮到我了！我要选…喂，那个红色的家伙，出列！
t_en: Hey, why not? It's my turn now! I'm going to pick... Hey, you, the red one over there! Step up!

t_ch: 到、到！
t_en: I... I'm here!

t_ch: 喊你了吗？无组织无纪律，回去！那个…就是，对，我选你。
t_en: Did I call you? Go back! I mean... yeah, you! Yeah, I'll choose you.

t_ch: 咦，要让人家来玩嘛？
t_en: Oh, me?

t_ch: 对的。呃，我想问…「你最喜欢的对象是谁？」
t_en: Yeah. Um... now I'm asking... Who's your favorite one to be with?

t_ch: 人家还没说要玩「真心话」呢。要回答也不是不行啦，人家最喜欢的对象是…是……
t_en: Hey, I haven't said I choose "Truth" yet. But fine, I guess I can answer this question. My favorite... is... well... you know...

t_ch: 呀！太难为情了！果然人家还是选「大冒险」吧！
t_en: Awww! It's too embarrassing! I'll go with "Dare"!

t_ch: …好吧。那就让你眨三次眼睛好了。
t_en: ...Alright, then... blink three times.

t_ch: 这么轻松？那人家开始咯？
t_en: That simple? Then I'll start?

t_ch: 接下来，人家要选…选…唔，选谁好呢？
t_en: Next, I'm going to choose... hmm... who should I choose?

t_ch: 为什么盯着我看？是要欺凌我吗？唉，就很郁闷，最后总会变成这样。
t_en: Why are you staring at me? Are you trying to bully me? *sigh* I knew it'd always end up like this.

t_ch: 才不是呢！哼，人家要选那边的大块头！
t_en: Hey, not at all! Hmph! I'll go with the big guy over there!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，是我吗？
t_en: Hee-hee, are you choosing me?

t_ch: （为什么指着这边…它看得到我？）
t_en: (Why is she pointing at me...? Wait, can she see me?)

t_ch: 人家问你呢，要选「真心话」还「大冒险」？
t_en: Hey, yeah you. "Truth" or "Dare"?

t_ch: （我的选择是……）
t_en: (My choice is...)

t_ch: …选「真心话」。
t_en: ...I'll go with "Truth."

t_ch: 行呀。人家要提问了哦——「有句著名的诗不是说过嘛，『自由』和『爱情』不能兼得，要是你的话，会选哪个呢？」
t_en: Alright, here's my question. You must have heard of this famous quote, "For liberty, I will sacrifice my love." If it were you, which one would you choose? Liberty or love?

t_ch: …「自由」
t_en: ...Liberty.

t_ch: …「爱情」
t_en: ...Love.

t_ch: 我会选择……
t_en: I'd choose...

t_ch: …选「大冒险」。
t_en: ...I'll go with "Dare."

t_ch: 行呀。人家要出题了哦——「你能不能…再表演一下那个？」
t_en: Alright, I'm going to ask you to do this. Can you... perform that trick again?
...That trick?

t_ch: 还要再来吗？
t_en: Again? Really?

t_ch: 对！就那个，快点，人家等不及了嘛。
t_en: Yeah, that trick! Come on. I can't wait.

t_ch: 我来试试吧……
t_en: Well, let me give it a try...

t_ch: 呵呵呵呵！不愧是你呀，人家好久没这么开心过了！
t_en: Hehehehe! I haven't had this much fun in ages!

t_ch: 漂亮！就喜欢看你这副模样！
t_en: Awesome! I love seeing you like this!

t_ch: 汪！实在是太好玩了！太好玩了哇！
t_en: Woof! It's so much fun! Yeah, so much fun!

t_ch: 果然拿别人寻开心才是喜剧。不过还是…哈哈，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: It's true that making fun of others is what makes comedy, but still... Hahaha... Hahahaha.

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎！笑得我肚子都疼了，怎么想出来的啊哈哈。可真有你的咔咔咔！
t_en: Mwahahaha! My stomach hurts from laughing so hard. How did you come up with that trick!? You're really something else! Mwahahaha!

t_ch: （…喂，所以那天你到底做了什么啊？）
t_en: (...So, what exactly did you do back then?)

t_ch: 你不需要知道。看到它们笑得这么欢快就够了。
t_en: You don't need to know the details. Seeing them laughing so hard is all you need to know.

t_ch: ——开始吧。
t_en: ...Here we go.

t_ch: 「这里有两扇门。」
t_en: "Here, you will find two doors."

t_ch: …我当然知道！
t_en: ...Of course I know that!

t_ch: 又开始废话了。
t_en: Let's just get straight to the point.

t_ch: 「左边的门代表*是*，右边的门代表*否*。」
t_en: "The door on the left means 'yes,' and the one on the right means 'no.'"

t_ch: 「请听好我的问题。」
t_en: "Riddle me this."

t_ch: 「和朋友共同度过的时光总是很快乐，你愿意放弃这样的生活吗？」
t_en: "It's always a pleasure to spend time with friends. Are you willing to give up this life?"

t_ch: 脱离家族的管控后，我们过得很快乐。
t_en: We've been leading a happy life ever since we broke free from The Family's control.

t_ch: 当然，受到噩梦的侵袭，大家的情绪都不稳定，冲突也在增加。
t_en: Of course, the Nightmare has left everyone emotionally unstable, and conflicts have increased.

t_ch: 至少，我们无须为任何事担忧害怕。
t_en: But at least we don't have to worry or fear anything.

t_ch: 我们很自由。我们可以决定自己要走什么样的路。
t_en: We are free now. We can decide which path we want to take.

t_ch: 回去吧，你还没有到达终点。
t_en: Now, go back. You haven't reached the end yet.

t_ch: 怎么搞的，没吃饱饭吗？连个骰子都转不好，养你们还有什么用？
t_en: What is wrong with you? Why should I keep your useless self around if you can't even roll the dice right!?

t_ch: 对、对不起…我会注意的，会注意的。您要…大还是小？
t_en: I—I'm sorry... I won't make this mistake ever again. Do you want... big or small?

t_ch: 问我大还是小？你脑子抽了吧！不会看桌上的局势？多数宾客在大的那边投注，就给我出小，在小的那边投注就给我出大，教你的都忘了？！
t_en: You're asking me big or small? Are you really that stupid!? Can't you see the situation on the table? If most guests bet on the big side, you roll small. Otherwise, roll big! Have you forgotten what I told you!?

t_ch: 每四轮让输最惨的那个人赢一次，遭到质询就谎称肚子痛，庄家可以不赢钱，但不能有任何宾客亏大钱。真是，看到你这欠揍的模样就来火。记住了吗？！
t_en: Let the most miserable loser win once every few rounds, and if someone questions us, find an excuse about having a tummy ache. The banker doesn't have to win all the time but must ensure nobody loses too much money! Do you remember now, you buffoon!?

t_ch: 我…我有点想上厕所。
t_en: I... I need to use the bathroom...

t_ch: 是让你对宾客撒谎，不是让你对我撒谎！**。鞭子拿来，看我不把你抽到听话为止！！
t_en: I'm asking you to lie to the guests, not to me! You fool! Bring me the whip, and I'll make sure you never forget it!!

t_ch: 汪！不要动手哇！
t_en: Woof! Please don't do that!

t_ch: 滚开！你这傻狗！整天只知道咬你那破汽水瓶，看个家都能丢东西。跑一边去！
t_en: Scram, you stupid dog! All you do is chew on your stupid soda bottles. You can't even protect the house without causing losses! Get lost, now!
...Waaaah!!

t_ch: 好可怕。人家…人家不敢看这种暴力场面呀。
t_en: That's so scary. I... I can't bear to watch such brutal scenes.

t_ch: 唉，我帮你遮住眼睛。
t_en: *sigh* Let me cover your eyes.

t_ch: 主人。要不今天就先…先算了，改天再……
t_en: Master, maybe... forget about it for today...? And do it some other time...?

t_ch: 我问你话了？我问你话了？啊？这下只是警告，再多嘴一句，我连你一起抽！！
t_en: Did I ask you anything? Huh? DID I!? This is just a warning. One more word, and you're gonna get it, too!
...I understand.

t_ch: **。正好我心情糟糕，就拿你们几个出气好了。明明是造出来给人消遣的工具，正经的事做不好，吃的喝的倒什么都不少，挨揍就是你们最后的用处！
t_en: Bah. I'm in a bad mood, so I'll take it out on you. You were made for entertainment, but you can't do anything right and keep wasting my money. You're good for being my practice dummy!

t_ch: 要你们有什么用，有人造没人教的玩意！砸碎了当零件卖都比现在强！
t_en: What's the point of owning you? Worthless pieces of junk! Why don't I just smash you up and sell you for parts!?

t_ch: 呜呜呜呜呜呜……
t_en: Boohoooo...

t_ch: 够了！停手吧！
t_en: Enough! Stop it!

t_ch: 欺负它们只会显得你很没用。
t_en: Taking it out on them only makes you look pathetic and useless.

t_ch: （…那家伙能听到我说话吗？）
t_en: (...Can he hear me?)

t_ch: 哈，大东西看到同伴被打，心里不舒服想插手是吧？我连你一起抽！
t_en: Hah, so now you think you're some kind of hero, you big nitwit? You're gonna get it, too!

t_ch: 以为自己很聪明？以为自己看透了很多道理？告诉你，没用！只要归我负责，我就能随便揍你们，你反抗不了，其他人也管不着！
t_en: You think you're smart? You think you've seen it all? Hah, nonsense! As long as I'm in charge, I can punish you as much as I want. Don't even think about fighting back or getting help from anyone!

t_ch: 非得抽到你怕为止！不怕怎么会听话？啊？！
t_en: I'll punish you until you're scared! You only listen when you're scared, right?

t_ch: 你想要掏出你最熟悉的那根球棒，却怎么都找不着。皮鞭重重打在你的身上，一下又一下，不算很痛，屈辱的滋味却是那般真切。
t_en: You try to grab your trusty baseball bat, but it's nowhere to be found. The whip keeps hitting you, not really causing much pain, but the humiliation is so real.

t_ch: （…你倒是说句话啊！难道就这么眼睁睁地看着它们被打吗？）
t_en: (...Say something! Are you just gonna stand there and watch them get hit?)

t_ch: 朝眼前的幻象大吼大叫，就能抹消过往，改变未来了么？
t_en: Do you really think you can erase the past and change the future by yelling at these illusions in front of you?

t_ch: 我说了，你就是当时的我。
t_en: Like I said, you are the former me.

t_ch: ——开始吧。
t_en: ...Here we go.

t_ch: 「还是那两扇门。」
t_en: "It's the same two doors as before."

t_ch: 就不能换个场景吗？
t_en: Can't we have a different scene for once?

t_ch: 熟悉的天花板。
t_en: Ah, the familiar ceiling.

t_ch: 「左边的门代表*是*，右边的门代表*否*。」
t_en: "The door on the left means 'yes,' and the one on the right means 'no.'"

t_ch: 「请听好我的问题。」
t_en: "Riddle me this."

t_ch: 「失去自由、任人摆布是件很痛苦的事，你想要逃离这样的生活吗？」
t_en: "Losing your freedom and being at the mercy of others is painful. Do you want to escape that kind of life?"

t_ch: 为家族服务的时候，我们过得很痛苦。
t_en: We led a miserable life when we served The Family.

t_ch: 虽然名叫「美梦剧团」，但美梦却从来都与我们无关。
t_en: We may be called the Sweet Dreams Troupe, but there's nothing sweet about our dreams.

t_ch: 现在，你选择帮助舒翁，把我们恢复成「美梦剧团」，知道这意味着什么吗？
t_en: Now that you've decided to help Siobhan and make us return to the Sweet Dreams Troupe, do you know what that means?

t_ch: 我们将重新过上遭人欺负的生活。没人会在乎工具的想法。
t_en: We'll lose our freedom and be at the mercy of others again. No one cares about what mere tools like us think.

t_ch: 拥有自由，我们才能获得幸福。
t_en: Breaking free is the only way to find happiness.

t_ch: 噩梦是家族的麻烦，却是我们唯一的机会。
t_en: The Nightmare may be a problem for The Family, but for us, it's our only chance to escape.

t_ch: 即使冒着发狂的风险，我也会在这个时候赌上全部。
t_en: Even if it drives me into a frenzy, I'm willing to risk everything at this point.

t_ch: 那…舒翁呢？
t_en: What about... Siobhan?

t_ch: 我没法反驳。
t_en: I can't argue with that.

t_ch: 回去吧，你的职责还没有结束。
t_en: Now, go back. You haven't fulfilled your duty yet.

t_ch: ——叫你们的负责人过来！
t_en: ...I wanna speak to your administrator!

t_ch: 房卡都能弄丢，我行李还在大堂，等着运进来呢，耽误了时间谁负责！
t_en: I can't believe you lost my room card! My luggage is still in the lobby! Who's gonna take responsibility if I'm delayed!?

t_ch: 汪！请稍等哇，我这就去帮您处理！
t_en: Woof! Please wait a moment. I'll go and handle it for you!

t_ch: 稍等？等多久？收房费的时候怎么就不等了，家族催款的时候怎么就不等了？
t_en: A moment? How long is that exactly? The Family never waits when they're charging for our room or collecting money, but I have to wait!?

t_ch: 我现在就要见你们的负责人！立刻，马上！不过来，你们谁都别想走！
t_en: I want to see the person in charge right now! No delays, no excuses! Or there will be consequences!

t_ch: 汪。可是…我们的管理员，他……
t_en: Woof... but our administrator...

t_ch: 哇咔咔。能不能…拜托不要告诉管理员，要不这样，我现在就飞去找前台……
t_en: Heh heh... Could we maybe... not involve the administrator? How about this? I'll go get the concierge right away...

t_ch: 解决起来很快的！很快的……
t_en: It'll be quick! I promise...

t_ch: 你在教我做事？我说要见，就得见着。带路的东西少插嘴！
t_en: Are you telling me what to do? Shut up! I want the administrator, now!

t_ch: 不管用什么方式，喊你们的管理员过来。否则…就等着瞧！
t_en: I don't care what you do. Just get your administrator. Or else!

t_ch: （这样的事经常发生吗？）
t_en: (Does this happen frequently?)

t_ch: 经常。几乎每天都会。
t_en: Very often. Pretty much every day.

t_ch: 就到这里吧。该有答案了。
t_en: Let's stop here. I think you have your answer now.

t_ch: 「最后的两扇门。」
t_en: "Now, here are the final doors."

t_ch: 到最后了么？
t_en: Is this the end?

t_ch: 「左边的门代表*是*，右边的门代表*否*。」
t_en: "The door on the left means 'yes,' and the one on the right means 'no.'"

t_ch: 「请听好我的问题。」
t_en: "Riddle me this."

t_ch: 「看完了过去的真实情景，你认为——我们应该重新变回『美梦剧团』吗？」
t_en: "Now that you have seen the real past, do you think we should return to the Sweet Dreams Troupe?"

t_ch: 「所谓*正常*，对我们而言只是奴役。」
t_en: "The so-called *normal life* is nothing more than just slavery for us."

t_ch: 「所谓*异常*，对我们而言才是自由。」
t_en: "To us, *abnormality* means freedom."

t_ch: 「既然没法享受『美梦』，不如彻底坠入『惊梦』。」
t_en: "If we can't enjoy the sweet dream, why not embrace the other side?"

t_ch: 「…你的选择是？」
t_en: "So... what's your choice?"

t_ch: 为什么还要问我？
t_en: Why are you asking me?

t_ch: 你不是想得很清楚了么？
t_en: Don't you already have an answer?
"Perhaps..."

t_ch: （奇怪，我怎么又回到这地方来了？）
t_en: (Weird, why am I back here again?)

t_ch: 还不快去通知？打算让我等到什么时候？！
t_en: What are you waiting for? How much longer do you expect me to wait!?

t_ch: …不好意思，让您久等了。我就是它们的管理者，有什么事尽管跟我谈吧。
t_en: ...I apologize for keeping you waiting. I'm the administrator here, so please let me know how I can help you today.

t_ch: 就是你？穿得像模像样，怎么就教不出几个靠谱的工具？是脑子不好使，还是水平太差，连这些破烂废都管不住？
t_en: You're the one? You might look respectable, but you can't even teach your tools how to do things right? Are you too stupid or too incompetent to manage this junk?

t_ch: 给您造成了困扰，非常抱歉，鸢尾花家系愿意赔……
t_en: I apologize for all the trouble caused. The Iris Family is willing to compensate you...

t_ch: 赔赔赔——你赔得起吗！你们的那点工资，连一个系统时的房费都付不起，还好意思说赔偿我？
t_en: Compensate me? Can you even afford it? With your measly salary, your compensation wouldn't even cover the room bill for one system hour. And you have the nerve to talk about compensating me?

t_ch: 这条狗弄丢了我的房卡，我要它给我找回来，就那张，其余的都不行。
t_en: Hey, this dog lost my room card, and I want it back. Just the one that's lost, not the others.

t_ch: 要是它找不回来，就换你去找！我不管你是趴地上，还是到处摇尾巴求人，反正我要……
t_en: If the dog can't find it, then you do it! I don't care if you have to beg, borrow, or steal, I'm going to...

t_ch: …滚出去。
t_en: ...Get out.

t_ch: 哈？你说什么？
t_en: Huh? What did you say?

t_ch: 我说…我要你从匹诺康尼消失。自己爬出去，还是我安排人送你，选一个吧。
t_en: I said... I want you to disappear from Penacony. You can make yourself scarce on your own, or I can arrange for it. Your choice.

t_ch: …不是，我可是家族邀请的贵宾，你敢这么对我说话？！
t_en: ...Wait, I'm a guest of honor invited by The Family. How dare you talk to me like this!?

t_ch: 呵…贵宾，什么贵宾？告诉你吧，我不仅敢这么对你说话——还敢对你动手。
t_en: Heh... a guest of honor, huh? Well, I'll let you in on a secret. Not only do I dare to talk to you like this, but I also can do something about it.

t_ch: 你口袋里插着的那个就是邀请函？给我看看…嗯，落款是「鸢尾花家系家主，梅芙恩」。
t_en: Oh, is that the invitation in your pocket? Let me check it... I see. It's signed by Maeven, head of the Iris Family.

t_ch: 梅芙恩的邀约啊，那撕掉也没关系吧？
t_en: An invitation from Maeven, huh? So it's okay if I tear it up. Don't you think?

t_ch: 装帧华丽的邀请函瞬间被撕成了碎屑，逐梦客的脸色也随之变得愈发苍白。
t_en: In the blink of an eye, the gorgeous invitation is torn to shreds. Colors drain from the Dreamchaser's face instantly.

t_ch: 好了。现在你不是受家族邀请的贵宾了——你就是个啥都不算的废物，可以滚了吧，只会欺负小东西的*废物*？
t_en: Well, then. Now that you're no longer a guest invited by The Family, you're just a nobody and a miserable *basket case* who bullies those little guys. Now, would you kindly vacate the premises?

t_ch: 你…你！我要去找人告状！你就是个疯子，实在是欺人太甚！
t_en: You... you're nuts! I'm going to file a complaint! There will be consequences!

t_ch: 别忘了要爬着出去噢？慢走不送！
t_en: And all that jazz. Exit's that way. Farewell!

t_ch: 这个人是谁哇，新来的管理员？
t_en: Who's that lady? A new administrator?

t_ch: 呜呜呜，我也不认识，不过…不过她刚才骂那个人的样子真的好帅嘎嘎嘎嘎！
t_en: Hmm, I don't know her, but... but she looked so cool when she was scolding that bad guy! Mwahahaha!

t_ch: 汪！我也觉得哇！
t_en: Woof! I agree, too!

t_ch: 你们是「美梦剧团」的成员吧？对这种垃圾别太客气，你越软弱，他们就越是得寸进尺。
t_en: You guys are members of the Sweet Dreams Troupe, right? Don't be too polite to varmints like that, because the more placating you are, the more they'll walk all over you.

t_ch: 下次记得，被欺负了及时去找你们的管理员啊。我先走啦，小家伙们。
t_en: And remember, if you get bullied next time, just come to me. Cheers, buddy.

t_ch: 是
t_en: Yes

t_ch: 否
t_en: No

t_ch: （这是…还没成为调饮师时候的舒翁？）
t_en: (Is this... Siobhan before she became a drinksmith?)

t_ch: 「这是我们和舒翁的第一次见面。」
t_en: "That was our first encounter with Siobhan."

t_ch: 你其实根本没有想好吧？
t_en: So, you haven't found your answer, right?

t_ch: 还是舍不得她？
t_en: You don't have the heart to leave her, do you?

t_ch: 「请听好我的问题——我们应该重新变回『美梦剧团』吗？」
t_en: "Now, please listen to my question: Do you think we should return to the Sweet Dreams Troupe?"

t_ch: 「所谓*正常*，对我们而言只是奴役。」
t_en: "The so-called *normal life* is nothing more than just slavery for us."

t_ch: 「所谓*异常*，对我们而言才是自由。」
t_en: "To us, *abnormality* means freedom."

t_ch: 但你失去了别的东西。
t_en: But you've lost something else.

t_ch: 还有其他值得珍惜的东西。
t_en: There's something else worth cherishing.

t_ch: （咦，这个房间在发生变化。）
t_en: (Huh? This room is changing.)

t_ch: 蓦然间，眼前的文字开始剧烈抖动起来，笔画纷纷脱落，瞬时组成了新的句子。
t_en: In an instant, the words start to tremble violently, the strokes cascading off, forming a new sentence entirely.

t_ch: 你抵达了最终的答案。
t_en: You've found the ultimate answer.

t_ch: 比「自由」更重要的东西…是「牵绊」。
t_en: What's even more important than freedom... is companionship.

t_ch: 这是你想要的答案吗？
t_en: Is that the answer you were hoping for?

t_ch: 我不知道。谁能知道呢。
t_en: I don't know. Well, who really knows?

t_ch: 你早就在这等我了吗？
t_en: Have you been waiting for me here for a while?

t_ch: 按原本的设想，你永远不可能到达这个房间。因为房卡被弄丢了。
t_en: Originally, reaching this room would have been impossible, because the room card was lost.

t_ch: 但有人帮你…帮我们解决了问题。
t_en: But someone helped you... and helped us out.

t_ch: 即使没拿到那把钥匙，你依旧打开了房门。
t_en: Even without the room card, you managed to open the door.

t_ch: 出去吧。这场思想实验已经有了结果。
t_en: It's time to leave. This thought experiment has come to fruition.

t_ch: （被传送到了奇怪的地方。这里是…梦境酒店？）
t_en: (He teleports me to a weird place. Is this the... hotel in the Dreamscape?)

t_ch: （…说是要听我的想法，自己的心声倒是完全暴露出来了。）
t_en: (...It said it wanted to hear my thoughts, but in the end, it poured out his own heart.)

t_ch: （我来帮它做出最后的决定吧。）
t_en: (Let me help him make his final decision.)

t_ch: 我在商店新买的收音机，送给你？
t_en: I bought a new radio at the store. Want it?

t_ch: 你不是总说酒吧里冷冷清清的，觉得很寂寞吗？我试了试，这个挺好用，无聊的时候就听听广播呗，鸢尾花家系出品的节目，不会无聊的。
t_en: You're always saying the lounge is cold and lonely, right? Well, check this out, it really does the trick. Try listening to the radio when you're bored. The programs from the Iris Family won't leave you bored.

t_ch: 汪！谢谢，我会珍惜的哇！
t_en: Woof! Thank you! I'll cherish it!

t_ch: 嗯…说到这个，把你捡回酒吧都过了这么久，是该给你取个名字了。
t_en: By the way... you've been staying in this lounge for quite a while. It's about time I give you a name.

t_ch: 你这么喜欢听广播，要不就叫…哈哈，我想到个有意思的！
t_en: Since you enjoy listening to the radio so much, why don't we call you... Oh! I've got something interesting in mind!

t_ch: 汪。是什么哇？
t_en: Woof! What's that?

t_ch: 从现在起，你就叫「司令」好了！
t_en: From now on, your name is "Mr. Bigwig"!

t_ch: 汪！我的名字是「司令」！好威风的名字，我好喜欢哇！
t_en: Woof! My name is "Mr. Bigwig"! An awesome name! I love it!

t_ch: ——向「司令」报告！近期家族封锁了梦境酒店，我打算再次前往深处探索，寻找是否还有像您这样，遭到抛弃的「美梦剧团」成员。请求指示！
t_en: ...Reporting to Mr. Bigwig! Recently, The Family sealed off the hotel in the Dreamscape. I'm planning to explore its depths again and see if there are any other Sweet Dreams Troupe members who have been abandoned like you. Requesting your instructions!

t_ch: 汪！我们出发吧！
t_en: Woof! Let's go!

t_ch: 走咯！
t_en: Yeah, let's go!

t_ch: 确实啊，虽然打算开家酒吧，但现在看来，缺的东西还有点多。正经的高档酒吧，空空荡荡的怎么能行！我去想想办法。
t_en: That's right. Although I want to open a lounge, I'm still lacking a fair few things now that I look at it. How can a proper upscale lounge be empty? I'll have to see what I can do.

t_ch: （我看到的是…舒翁记忆里的场景？这时候酒吧里还只有司令。）
t_en: (Did I just witness... a scene in Siobhan's memory? There was only Mr. Bigwig in the lounge at this time.)

t_ch: （拿到了一块拼图碎片，后面或许派得上用场。）
t_en: (Got a jigsaw piece. It probably will come in handy later.)

t_ch: （听刚才的谈话，舒翁打算去梦境酒店深处，继续寻找其余「美梦剧团」。先跟过去看看吧。）
t_en: (From what I've just heard, Siobhan is planning to explore deeper into the hotel in the Dreamscape to search for the rest of the Sweet Dreams Troupe. Let's follow her.)

t_ch: 嘎嘎嘎…这是个什么鬼地方，睡了个觉，醒来人全没了，还到处都是…呜呜，都是怪物！
t_en: Hmm... what's this terrible place? I woke up and found myself here, with nobody but... *sob* monsters!

t_ch: 真是倒霉！霉到家了！呜，紧张就想要上厕所…可这哪有呢？
t_en: What bad luck! Oh, this is terrible! Oooh, I need a bathroom when I'm nervous... but where is it?

t_ch: 汪！是和我一起工作过的骰子先生哇！
t_en: Woof! It's my peer Mr. Dice!

t_ch: 四加二减三的结果…是大还是小？
t_en: Four plus two minus three... is the result big or small?

t_ch: 大…是大。
t_en: Big... it's big.

t_ch: 五乘四再减去二，最后除以六…得到的结果是大还是小？
t_en: Five times four minus two, and then divided by six... is the result big or small?

t_ch: 小…是小。
t_en: Small... it's small.

t_ch: 八的平方除以四，减去四，再除以四…得到的结果是大还是小？
t_en: Eight squared divided by four, minus four, and then divided by four... is the result big or small?

t_ch: 我…我肚子有点疼，想上厕所。
t_en: I... I have a little tummy ache and I need to use the bathroom.

t_ch: 汪！这些题目，我完全算不出来哇！
t_en: Woof! These questions are just too hard!

t_ch: 算不出来才正常。这种小型的「美梦剧团」，本身就没有很强的思考能力，刚才的问题只是为了测试它是否有攻击性罢了，看来意识还挺清楚，没有被噩梦侵蚀太深。
t_en: It's natural if you can't handle these questions. Small-sized Sweet Dreams Troupe members don't have good computation abilities. I asked it those questions just to see if it's becoming combative, and luckily, it seems like the nightmare corruption on it's still not too deep.

t_ch: 不介意的话，跟我回去呗？
t_en: Would you come back with me, if you don't mind?

t_ch: 回去…回哪里？
t_en: Come back... but where?

t_ch: 我在梦境酒店的深处开了家酒吧，专门用来收容像你们这样遭到抛弃的…「美梦剧团」，不用工作，开开心心生活就好。唯一的要求——不准打架。
t_en: I've opened a lounge in the depths of the hotel in the Dreamscape for those abandoned... Sweet Dreams Troupe members like you. There, you won't have to work, just live happily. The only rule is — no fighting.

t_ch: 愿意来吗？继续留在封锁的酒店里，你会很危险，没有其他人的陪伴…你也会感到孤独。
t_en: Will you join us? It could be dangerous for you to stay in this sealed hotel all alone, and you'll... feel lonely without any company.

t_ch: 但和我们共同生活，你会拥有「家人」。
t_en: But if you join us, you'll have a "Family."

t_ch: …家人。
t_en: ...Family.

t_ch: 那…我、我可以随时去上厕所吗？
t_en: Then... c—can I take bathroom breaks whenever I want?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，当然可以，你可以随时去做你想做的任何事，不用受任何人的限制，也不会再有人借着管教的名义欺负你们。我不会允许之前的情况再次发生。
t_en: Hahaha, of course you can. You can do whatever you want without any restrictions, and there won't be any bullying in the name of discipline. I won't allow what happened before to happen again.

t_ch: ——有兴趣就跟上来吧！我们该回家了。
t_en: ...Follow up if you're interested! It's time to go home.

t_ch: （好像听到了黑桃的声音……）
t_en: (Think I hear Spade's voice...)

t_ch: （舒翁是要带着黑桃回酒吧吗？跟回去看看吧。）
t_en: (Is Siobhan bringing Spade back to the lounge? Let's follow them and see.)

t_ch: 汪！酒吧的成员增加了哇！舒翁也给骰子先生取个名字吧！
t_en: Woof! We've got a new partner! Siobhan, let's give Mr. Dice a name, too!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎！我、我想要个最响亮的名字，听着就能把人吓到腿软的那种！
t_en: Mwahaha! I... I want the coolest name you can think of, the kind that scares people just by hearing it!

t_ch: 以前写剧本的时候，我就特别讨厌给角色取名字，想想啊…你的骰子上印着图案，长得又很黑，干脆就叫你「黑桃」好了。
t_en: Well, I was terrible at naming characters when I wrote scripts... I see you have some spades on your die, so maybe I'll just call you "Spade"?

t_ch: 呜咕，这么随便的吗。
t_en: Whoa... that's so casual.

t_ch: 汪！我觉得这个名字很棒哇，很适合你！
t_en: Woof! I think it's a great name! It suits you!

t_ch: 咔咔？哪里适合我了！你这只…傻…傻狗！呼哈哈哈哈，傻狗！
t_en: Mwa? How does it suit me? You... you silly... silly dog! Hah hah, silly dog!

t_ch: ——停！说过不准在这里打架。这么有精神，来帮我布置酒吧啊。
t_en: Hey, stop! No fighting here. If you're so energetic, come help me set up the lounge.

t_ch: （拿到了第二块碎片…或许我可以尝试完成这幅拼图。）
t_en: (Found the second jigsaw piece... Maybe I can attempt to finish the jigsaw.)

t_ch: …款式够精致，和整体的氛围也很搭，看起来终于有点酒吧的样子了。
t_en: ...The design is sophisticated, and it fits well with the overall atmosphere. Finally, it's starting to look like a lounge.

t_ch: 是不是还缺了点什么？总感觉哪个地方不太对呢。
t_en: But there's still something missing. Something just doesn't feel right.

t_ch: 汪！因为还没有顾客哇！要是坐满了光临的客人，就会更像了！
t_en: Woof! It's because there are no customers yet! It would feel more like a lounge if it were filled with customers!

t_ch: 不对！嘻嘻…哪有这么干净的吧台，要堆满那个…纸钞和筹码才有感觉嘛。
t_en: Wait! Hee-hee... what kind of counter is so clean? It has to be piled with... paper money and chips to bring out the vibes!

t_ch: 啊，我明白了。
t_en: Ah, I get it.

t_ch: 我忘了准备调制饮品用的材料。
t_en: I forgot to prepare the ingredients for mixing the drink.

t_ch: 如果你还打算躲在这个破酒吧，陪那群怪物玩过家家游戏的话——就别怪鸢尾花家系没给你留位置！
t_en: If you're planning to keep hanging out in this shabby lounge and play house with those monsters... don't blame the Iris Family for not saving a spot for you!

t_ch: 你、你实在是…太不把自己的前途当回事了！
t_en: Seriously, you... you're not taking your future seriously enough!

t_ch: 家族的地位，还有那些鲜花和欢呼对我有什么用？艾米绮，是你陷得太深了。
t_en: The status within The Family, the superficial flowers and applauses — What can I do with any of that? You're the one who's gotten too caught up in it, Amiky.

t_ch: 司令。出发吧。
t_en: Let's go, Mr. Bigwig.

t_ch: 汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: （完成拼图后，记忆的时间线发生了推进。）
t_en: (After completing the jigsaw, the timeline of the memory advances.)

t_ch: （舒翁还是打算留在这里，继续搜寻梦境酒店深处被遗弃的「美梦剧团」。）
t_en: (Siobhan still plans to stay here and continue searching for the abandoned Sweet Dreams Troupe deeper in the hotel in the Dreamscape.)

t_ch: （完成那副拼图后，酒吧内出现了相应的器具摆设。）
t_en: (After completing the jigsaw, the respective item appeared in the lounge.)

t_ch: （大概明白了，我在观看的是舒翁和怪物们相遇、共同建设酒吧的过程，只有最重要的时刻得到了保存。）
t_en: (I can understand it now. I'm currently watching the process of Siobhan meeting with the monsters and building the lounge together. Only the most important moments were preserved.)

t_ch: （这里是舒翁的内心世界，她将过往袒露出来，是为了重获怪物们的信任。）
t_en: (This is Siobhan's inner world. She's revealing her past to regain the monsters' trust.)

t_ch: （我来帮她将全部场景都展现出来吧。）
t_en: (Let me help her reveal the whole scenario.)

t_ch: （…视野开始变暗，是要跳跃到下一段记忆了吗？）
t_en: (...The field of vision is getting darker. Is it time to jump to the next memory?)

t_ch: 美梦不过是随便借个名字，用幻想吹出的肥皂泡。呵！爱情皆是虚假，激情生于愚昧！
t_en: Sweet dreams are as fickle as fantasy-filled soap bubbles. Hah! Love stems from lies, and passion arises from ignorance!

t_ch: 你们…是特意过来欺负我的吗？
t_en: Are you... here just to bully me?

t_ch: 没有哇！舒翁想要帮助你！
t_en: Not at all! Siobhan wants to help you!

t_ch: 你曾经是「美梦剧团」的成员吧，被家族抛弃在梦境酒店里了吗？
t_en: You were part of the Sweet Dreams Troupe, weren't you? Did The Family abandon you in the Dreamscape?

t_ch: 唉，我从来都是孑然一身，若是未曾抱有过期待，何来抛弃？
t_en: *sigh* I have always been on my lonesome. If the expectation was never envisaged, how would one be considered abandoned?

t_ch: …正常点说话。
t_en: ...Please speak like a normal person.

t_ch: 我在不远处开了家酒吧，在那里，不会有任何人欺负你，还能遇到和你有着相同处境的「美梦剧团」。你愿意跟我回去吗？
t_en: I've set up a lounge nearby where no one will bully you. There, you can meet others from the Sweet Dreams Troupe who are in the same situation as you. Will you come with me?

t_ch: 真的…没人欺负我？
t_en: Are you sure... no one will bully me there?

t_ch: 没有。至少我不允许。
t_en: Yes, I won't allow it, at least.

t_ch: 我还需要参与戏剧，扮演其中的角色吗？
t_en: Do I still need to participate in plays and act out roles?

t_ch: 应该不用。看你的兴趣？
t_en: No, you don't. Or perhaps you can, if you're interested?

t_ch: 真的…真的…不会有人欺负我？
t_en: Are you really... really sure... no one will bully me?

t_ch: …你到底是希望有，还是不希望有啊。我要走了，想跟我回家吗？
t_en: ...Are you actually expecting a "yes" from me? I'm leaving. Do you want to come with me or not?

t_ch: 带我走吧！
t_en: Take me with you!

t_ch: （又得到了拼图碎片…先收着吧。舒翁该带忧郁派回酒吧了。）
t_en: (Found another jigsaw piece... Let's keep it first. It's about time Siobhan brings Melancholy back to the lounge.)

t_ch: 我听说这里有家酒吧。
t_en: I heard there was a lounge here.

t_ch: 汪！还是第一次有怪物找上门来哇！
t_en: Woof! This is the first time a monster actively shows up here!

t_ch: 是啊，我就是这家酒吧的店主。看你的模样，似乎完全没有受到噩梦的影响？
t_en: Yeah, I'm the owner of this lounge. Judging by your appearance, it seems like the nightmare hasn't affected you, right?

t_ch: 至少到目前，我的思想无比清晰。
t_en: At least my mind has been unclouded so far.

t_ch: 告诉我，你到处将这些遭到遗弃的怪物带回来，目的是什么？
t_en: Tell me, what's your purpose in bringing back these abandoned monsters?

t_ch: 和你没关系吧？既然你能避免梦境坍塌的影响，就赶紧离开这里，找个安全的地方待着。
t_en: It's none of your business, is it? Now that you've managed to avoid the impact of the collapsing Dreamscape, leave and find a safe place.

t_ch: 那为什么，你还留在梦境酒店里？
t_en: Then why are you still staying at the Dreamscape yourself?

t_ch: 我还有事情没做完。
t_en: I have unfinished business.

t_ch: 听我说，即使你现在没有发狂的迹象，但只要是「美梦剧团」，就不可避免地会受到噩梦的侵袭，趁现在神智还清楚，早点逃到安全的地方。
t_en: Listen, even if you're not showing signs of frenzy now, as part of the Sweet Dreams Troupe, the nightmare will eventually corrupt you. So leave now and find a safe place while you still have your sanity.

t_ch: 但同样，只要是「美梦剧团」，就没有安全的地方。
t_en: But similarly, as long as someone's part of the Sweet Dreams Troupe, there's no actual safe place for them.

t_ch: 让我去你的酒吧里看看。
t_en: Show me your lounge.

t_ch: 汪！我们回来了！
t_en: Woof! We're back!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎嘎！是新来的朋友。听好了，在这家酒吧里，你们都得按我说的去做。
t_en: Mwahahahaha! Some new friends! Alright, listen up, in this lounge, you all do what I say.

t_ch: 我说啥，就得做啥。明白吗？明白了就乖乖给我…嘻嘻，刷厕所怎么样？
t_en: Whenever I give an order, you follow it, got it? Now, if you understand, be a good troupe member and... clean the toilets, what do you say?

t_ch: 唉，果然，最后还是要欺负我。
t_en: *sigh* I knew someone would bully me in the end.

t_ch: 胡说什么呢。酒吧里没有那种规矩，自在点就好。对了，还得给你们俩取名字。
t_en: Stop your nonsense. Relax, there are no such rules in this lounge. Oh, I need to give you guys names.

t_ch: 要不…你就叫「忧郁派」吧！
t_en: How about... I just call you "Melancholy"?

t_ch: 唉，这个名字不适合我。你们看，我既不忧郁，也不怎么会写诗。
t_en: *sigh* That name doesn't really fit me. I mean, I'm not melancholic, and I'm not poetic at all.

t_ch: 更何况，忧郁并非是情绪，而是与这个世界相处的态度。
t_en: Besides, melancholy encompasses more than just an emotion: It's an attitude towards the world.

t_ch: 汪！听听你说的话！这个名字非你莫属了哇。
t_en: Woof! You really should hear what you just said! That name suits you perfectly.

t_ch: 嘻嘻。莫非这就是那些宾客嘴里经常会骂的…雅痞？哇咔咔，这两个字是这么写的嘛？
t_en: Hee-hee, perhaps this is what those customers call... yuppies? Mwahaha! Y—u—p—p—i—e? Is that how it's spelled?

t_ch: 到你了。让我想想啊……
t_en: It's your turn now. Let me see...

t_ch: 我有名字，叫做「小星星」。
t_en: Wait, I already have a name. It's Starlet.

t_ch: 汪？
t_en: Woof?

t_ch: 朋友，你把大家都给整沉默了。
t_en: I... I don't even know what to say, my friend.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈！不是吧，这么大的块头，名字却叫…小星星？这是什么奇怪的审美哈哈哈！
t_en: Hahahaha! Come on, big guy! Are you seriously called... Starlet? What kind of quirky style is that? Hahahaha!

t_ch: 尊重！不要嘲笑别人的名字！
t_en: Show some respect! Don't make fun of people's names!

t_ch: 抱歉。我没有取笑的意思，但这个实在是…太好笑了哈哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Sorry, didn't mean to poke fun, but it's just... too hilarious! Hahahahaha!

t_ch: 小星星…小星星，请问你会眨眼睛吗？一闪一闪，亮晶晶的那种哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Starlet... Do you twinkle, Starlet? Twinkle, twinkle, little star... Hahahaha!

t_ch: 听着，我是会生气的！吼——！！！
t_en: Hey now, you won't like to see me mad! Roar...!!!

t_ch: 好了好了。我们继续来布置酒吧，既然有新成员加入，效率应该能提升不少。
t_en: Alright. Let's get back to setting up the lounge. With new members on board, we'll finish it up in no time.

t_ch: 麻烦忧郁派和…小星星来帮忙哈哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: I need your help, Melancholy and... Starlet... Hahahahahaha!

t_ch: 哎。
t_en: Hey!

t_ch: （拿到了全部碎片，可以去完成拼图了。这次拼出来的会是什么呢？）
t_en: (Found all the jigsaw pieces. It's time to complete the jigsaw. Wonder what will be the complete image?)

t_ch: 汪！我们的酒吧建好了哇！好漂亮，好气派！！
t_en: Woof! Our lounge is all set and ready to roll! It looks so awesome and grand!!!
